story.iWant: a single command that initializes the complete development environment
story.soThat: onboarding is as simple as running one script

tasks.task: Test error handling (e.g., Docker not running)

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: Script performs: Docker Compose startup, health check verification, Git hooks installation, dependency download
scripts/health-check.sh validates all services are ready (PostgreSQL connectable, Keycloak realm exists, Redis responding)
scripts/seed-data.sh loads test data (Keycloak users, sample tenants)
scripts/install-git-hooks.sh installs pre-commit and pre-push hooks
./scripts/init-dev.sh completes successfully in <5 minutes on clean system
All services accessible after script completion
Script provides clear progress output and error messages

doc.title: Product Requirements Document
doc.section: FR001: Development Environment Setup and Infrastructure
doc.snippet: The system shall provide one-command initialization of complete local development stack (PostgreSQL, Keycloak, Redis, Prometheus, Grafana) with automatic migrations and seed data, plus Gradle multi-module monorepo structure with Spring Modulith boundary enforcement and Docker Compose templates.

doc.title: Product Requirements Document
doc.snippet: The system shall provide hot reload, breakpoint debugging in event handlers, local feature flags, and pre-commit validation with automated fixes.

doc.title: Decision Architecture
doc.snippet: EAF v1.0 reuses validated prototype structure from production-proven reference implementation. Foundation Strategy provides one-command setup with Docker Compose stack initialization, health checks, and seed data.

doc.title: Epic 1 Technical Specification
doc.snippet: One-command initialization script (./scripts/init-dev.sh) with health checks and seed data. Docker Compose stack includes PostgreSQL 16.10, Keycloak 26.4.2, Redis 7.2, Prometheus, Grafana 12.2. Init script workflow: Start services, wait for health, load seed data, install Git hooks, download dependencies.

doc.title: Story 1.5: Docker Compose Development Stack
doc.snippet: Docker Compose stack fully functional with 5 services running and healthy. PostgreSQL with uuid-ossp and eaf schema, Keycloak with eaf realm and 3 test users, Redis responding, Prometheus and Grafana configured. Health checks operational for all services.

doc.title: Project Development Guidelines
doc.snippet: Initialize development stack with ./scripts/init-dev.sh. Script performs Docker Compose startup, service validation, and dependency setup. Target completion time <5 minutes on clean system.

doc.title: Project README
doc.snippet: Project README with development setup instructions and service endpoints reference.

artifact.reason: Complete Docker Compose configuration with 5 services (postgres, redis, keycloak, prometheus, grafana). Implemented in Story 1.5. Health checks already configured for all services.
PostgreSQL initialization scripts with uuid-ossp extension and Axon Framework event store schema. Executed automatically on container startup.
Keycloak realm configuration with eaf-api client and 3 test users. Imported automatically via --import-realm flag.
Prometheus scrape configuration for EAF application and Keycloak metrics.
Environment variable template with security warnings. Contains all configurable ports and credentials for local development.
Gradle wrapper script for downloading dependencies. Used in init-dev.sh to pre-fetch project dependencies.
Scripts directory exists but only contains .gitkeep. All init scripts (init-dev.sh, health-check.sh, seed-data.sh, install-git-hooks.sh) need to be created.
Git hooks directory contains only sample hooks. Pre-commit and pre-push hooks need to be installed by install-git-hooks.sh script.

gradle.note: All Gradle dependencies managed via gradle/libs.versions.toml. Gradle wrapper (./gradlew) downloads dependencies automatically.

docker-compose.note: All services defined in docker-compose.yml. Docker Compose 2.40.3+ required. Health checks configured for all services.

tool.name: docker-compose
tool.description: Docker Compose v2+ for multi-container orchestration

tool.name: pg_isready
tool.description: PostgreSQL client tool for health checks (provided by postgres container)

tool.name: redis-cli
tool.description: Redis client tool for health checks (provided by redis container)

tool.name: curl
tool.description: HTTP client for health checks (optional, shell-based alternative used)

system-tools.note: Docker and Docker Compose must be installed on host. Service-specific tools (pg_isready, redis-cli) available inside containers.

constraints.constraint: Scripts must be POSIX-compliant shell (#!/bin/bash or #!/bin/sh) for cross-platform compatibility
Scripts must use 'set -e' to fail fast on errors
Health checks must implement retry logic with timeout (max 2 minutes recommended)
Script execution time target: <5 minutes on clean system
All scripts must be made executable with chmod +x
Scripts must provide clear progress output and informative error messages
Health check validation: PostgreSQL connectable, Keycloak realm exists, Redis responding
Git hooks installation must not overwrite existing custom hooks (warn user if hooks exist)
Seed data must be idempotent (safe to run multiple times)
Scripts location: ./scripts/ directory at project root
Docker Compose must be installed and Docker daemon must be running (validate before attempting startup)
All environment variables should use defaults from .env.example

interface.name: Docker Compose CLI
interface.description: Starts all services in detached mode. Services start in dependency order with health checks.

interface.name: PostgreSQL Health Check
interface.description: PostgreSQL readiness check. Returns 0 when database is accepting connections.

interface.name: Keycloak Health Check
interface.description: Keycloak management health endpoint. Returns HTTP 200 when realm is loaded and ready.

interface.name: Redis Health Check
interface.description: Redis ping command. Returns PONG when service is ready.

interface.name: Gradle Wrapper
interface.description: Downloads all project dependencies to local Gradle cache. Uses --quiet flag to reduce output noise.

tests.standards:
Shell script validation for Story 1.6 focuses on manual integration testing and error handling verification rather than automated unit tests. Constitutional TDD principles apply through validation scripts and comprehensive manual testing protocols.
**Testing Approach for Shell Scripts:** - **Manual Integration Testing:** Execute scripts on clean system and verify outcomes - **Error Handling Tests:** Validate script behavior when prerequisites fail (Docker not running, services unreachable) - **Idempotency Tests:** Verify scripts can be run multiple times safely - **Performance Validation:** Measure execution time against <5 minute target - **Output Validation:** Verify clear progress messages and error reporting
**EAF Testing Standards:** - Framework: Kotest 6.0.4 (for future Kotlin-based test automation if needed) - Coverage: Manual verification of all acceptance criteria - Quality Gates: Scripts must pass ktlint/shellcheck before commit (Story 1.10 Git hooks) - CI/CD: Automated execution in Story 1.9 pipeline
**Reference:** docs/architecture/test-strategy.md (Constitutional TDD), docs/architecture/coding-standards.md (Script standards)
tests.locations: scripts/init-dev.sh - Main initialization script scripts/health-check.sh - Service health validation scripts/seed-data.sh - Test data loader scripts/install-git-hooks.sh - Git hooks installer .git/hooks/pre-commit - Pre-commit hook (installed by script) .git/hooks/pre-push - Pre-push hook (installed by script)

ideas.test: **Happy Path - Clean System Initialization** Execute ./scripts/init-dev.sh on clean system (no services running, no .env file). Verify: - Docker Compose starts all 5 services - Health checks pass for all services - Seed data loads successfully - Git hooks installed - Gradle dependencies downloaded - Completion message displayed - Total execution time <5 minutes

**Service Health Check Validation** After init-dev.sh completes, manually verify each service: - PostgreSQL: Connect with psql -h localhost -U eaf_user -d eaf - PostgreSQL: Query eaf schema tables (SELECT * FROM information_schema.tables WHERE table_schema='eaf') - Keycloak: Access http://localhost:8080, verify eaf realm exists - Keycloak: Login with test user credentials from realm-export.json - Redis: Execute redis-cli ping, expect PONG response - Prometheus: Access http://localhost:9090/-/ready, expect HTTP 200 - Grafana: Access http://localhost:3000/api/health, expect HTTP 200

**Seed Data Verification** Verify seed-data.sh creates test data correctly: - Keycloak: 3 test users exist (admin, viewer, tenant-b-admin) - Keycloak: Users have correct roles and tenant_id attributes - PostgreSQL: Sample tenants exist in database - Data can be queried successfully

**Git Hooks Installation** Verify install-git-hooks.sh installs hooks correctly: - .git/hooks/pre-commit exists and is executable - .git/hooks/pre-push exists and is executable - Pre-commit hook runs ktlint on staged files - Pre-push hook runs Detekt and tests - Hooks can be manually triggered (git commit, git push --dry-run)

**Performance Target Validation** Measure execution time on clean system: - Start timer before ./scripts/init-dev.sh - Record completion time - Verify total time <5 minutes - Identify any bottlenecks if target missed

**Progress Output and Error Messages** Verify script provides clear feedback: - Progress messages for each major step (Starting services, Waiting for health, Loading data, etc.) - Success indicators (✅ or similar) for completed steps - Final summary with service URLs and next steps - Clear error messages if any step fails

**Error Handling - Docker Not Running** Test script behavior when Docker daemon is not running: - Stop Docker daemon - Execute ./scripts/init-dev.sh - Verify clear error message: "Docker is not running. Please start Docker and try again." - Verify script exits with non-zero status code - Verify no partial state left behind

**Error Handling - Service Fails to Start** Test script behavior when a service fails health check: - Simulate service failure (e.g., modify docker-compose.yml with invalid config) - Execute ./scripts/init-dev.sh - Verify timeout after max retry period (2 minutes) - Verify clear error message identifying which service failed - Verify script exits gracefully

**Idempotency - Re-run Scripts** Verify scripts can be run multiple times safely: - Execute ./scripts/init-dev.sh successfully - Execute ./scripts/init-dev.sh again (services already running) - Verify script detects running services - Verify script completes successfully without errors - Verify no data duplication or corruption

**Git Hooks - Existing Hooks Warning** Test install-git-hooks.sh behavior with existing hooks: - Manually create custom .git/hooks/pre-commit with content - Execute ./scripts/install-git-hooks.sh - Verify warning message about existing hooks - Verify script asks for confirmation before overwriting - Verify existing hooks can be preserved if user declines
